content.problemStatement:
You have a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) rooted at vertex 111. Each vertex contains a decimal digit; let's denote the digit in vertex iii by did_idi‚Äã.

A subtree is a non-empty subgraph satisfying the following condition: for any vertex vvv, if vvv belongs to subtree SSS, then the parent of vvv also belongs to SSS. Note that each subtree contains the root. The vertices in any subtree SSS are sorted in DFS-order, i.e. using the following algorithm:


let's denote a current vertex by ccc; initially, the current vertex is the root
when processing vertex ccc:

ccc is the next vertex in DFS-order
consider all sons of ccc which belong to SSS, sorted in increasing order
for each of these sons, change the current vertex ccc to that son and process it recursively


For any subtree SSS with kkk vertices, we define the digit string D(S)D(S)D(S) as a string with length kkk; for each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string is the digit in the iii-th vertex of SSS (in DFS-order). D(S)D(S)D(S) may also be considered an integer in decimal representation.

Let's define maxDigit(x)\mathrm{maxDigit}(x)maxDigit(x) as the largest decimal digit in a positive integer xxx. Consider the following infinite recurrent sequence aaa:


a1=1a_1 = 1a1‚Äã=1
an=an‚àí1+maxDigit(an‚àí1)a_n = a_{n-1} + \mathrm{maxDigit}(a_{n-1})an‚Äã=an‚àí1‚Äã+maxDigit(an‚àí1‚Äã) for each n>1n \gt 1n>1
For example, the first few members of this sequence are 1,2,4,8,16,221, 2, 4, 8, 16, 221,2,4,8,16,22.

Next, let's call a subtree SSS good if D(S)D(S)D(S) occurs in sequence aaa. You should find the number of good subtrees of the given tree. Since this number may be large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points):** di=2d_i = 2di‚Äã=2 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the following N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
The last line contains NNN space-separated integers d1,d2,‚Ä¶,dNd_1, d_2, \ldots, d_Nd1‚Äã,d2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,dN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of good subtrees modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
